---
id: "19.2.9"
title: "HCI Data Resiliency and Rebuild Progress (Post-failure recovery)"
criticality: "critical"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.2.9 · HCI Data Resiliency and Rebuild Progress (Post-failure recovery)

## Description

Correlates post-failure resync, rebuild, and data-redundancy telemetry from vSAN, Nutanix AOS, HyperFlex, and optional Azure Stack HCI with SLA rows from the `rebuild_owner` lookup, estimates remaining bytes, rebuild throughput, an ETA, time-in-rebuild since the first resyncing poll or a vCenter event, and a count of objects in exposed states, so operators can see when the cluster is still in the one-more-failure danger window after UC-19.2.5 has already tripped.

## Value

A slow rebuild is not a single-disk story, it is calendar risk: finance batches, EHR, and factory floor historians all assume the second disk can fail tomorrow without losing quorum. This UC makes the resync bar, the ETA, and the fault-tolerance shortfall line up on one row with the owning service, so the storage team, app owners, and incident bridge share the same numbers as vSphere, Prism, HX, and WAC, before a second fault turns a resync into a recovery exercise.

## Implementation

Deploy the TAs, wire HEC pollers for the vSAN, Curator, HyperFlex, and optional Azure JSON lines, and ship `rebuild_owner.csv` with a `transforms.conf` `rebuild_owner` stanza. Paste the `spl` into a report, confirm each sourcetype returns rows in dev, set the time picker to match `earliest=-2h@h` for the interactive proof, then schedule a five- or fifteen-minute alert with throttling on `cluster_name` and `severity_bucket`, routing the mapping in Step 4.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites

In hyperconverged storage, a failed disk or node does not end the day by itself. Arrays mirror or erasure-code data, then schedule background work: read remaining fragments, re-stripe objects, and reapply policy so that a second loss in a related fault domain does not become data loss. The post-failure window has a life cycle: failure is detected, the cluster marks objects as non-compliant, rebuild or resync work starts, and only when the last missing replica or parity slice is in place is the system back to the fault tolerance the business bought. That is the subject of this UC, not the first alarm that a disk is missing (covered in UC-19.2.5) and not the pre-failure placement story (UC-19.2.16) and not the free capacity question (UC-19.2.27) or the CVM process map (UC-19.2.7).

Why rebuild speed matters: while bytes remain in flight, you are one additional fault away from unrecoverable data for some objects, or from stretched-cluster split-brain edge cases, depending on design. Slower resync is not a vanity metric, it is clock time in which a second power event, NIC storm, or firmware bug can line up with the first. Throughput bounces with foreground I/O, cache tier, and network back-pressure, so a tiny delta over two pollers is a noisy way to measure GB per second, which is why the canonical SPL keeps raw samples and then smooths a windowed minimum start time and a `streamstats` delta of remaining bytes in the time-sorted run.

vSAN object health, from the public API surface, buckets objects into `healthy` rows plus stress states: `nonAvailabilityRelatedReconfiguration`, `nonAvailabilityRelatedIncompliance`, `dataMove` (rebalance or policy moves), and `reduced` availability where policy still has copies but a fault domain is gone. A compact mapping for this UC: treat `reduced` plus non-availability family counts that still appear in the resyncing queue as exposed objects for alerting; `healthy` with zero bytes in `resyncing-objects` is compliant. If your operations team has signed off on a narrower definition, change only the `exp_obj` expression in the VMware branch, not the outer severity contract.

Nutanix AOS shows `data_resiliency_status` on the `nutanix:cluster` stream from `Splunk_TA_nutanix` as values such as `OK`, `kCriticalAttentionNeeded`, and `kFailureDetected`. The cluster feed does not, by itself, list every Stargate background task, because Curator runs scheduled map-reduce style scans, scores extents, and hands repair work to Stargate, which is why you add `nutanix:cluster:curator` with an explicit HEC poller to `/PrismGateway/services/rest/v1/curator/jobs` and keep `nutanix:cluster:alerts` for `RebuildSlowProgress`, `OplogStoreDriveCriticallyLow`, and `RfReplication` so Splunk can light up the same titles operations already chases in Prism.

Cisco HyperFlex encodes a storage controller layer that ties SCVM, cluster personality, and the CIP-VFB path for replica placement. The REST cluster record exposes `data_redundancy_state`, `replication_factor`, and a coarse `state` that may lag a few minutes behind the alarm bus; rebuild orchestration and mirror completion still surface in `cisco:hyperflex:alarm` for titles that include rebuild or resync language.

For Azure Stack HCI, Windows Server Storage Spaces Direct exposes one-row-per-job `Get-StorageJob` output when regeneration or repair is running. Once the job is gone, the cmdlet returns nothing, which is not proof that the pool was always healthy, only that the job finished, so the optional HEC line remains valuable for the active window, not long-term state history on its own.

Why ETA wobbles: vSAN and Nutanix both prioritise hot working sets, cold data, and small components differently. Early in a rebuild, your derived throughput is almost zero for several polling intervals, so ETA from `bytes / rate` looks infinite. The SPL therefore keeps `streamstats` on remaining bytes, compares against a wider window, and you should not treat the first one or two post-failure events as a commitment without matching the vendor UI’s cumulative bar.

Governance: keep `lookups/rebuild_owner.csv` with a `rebuild_owner` transform so both `| inputlookup rebuild_owner` and `| lookup rebuild_owner` resolve. Schema: `cluster_name` (string that must match the normalised `cluster_name` the pollers send), `vendor` (vmware, nutanix, cisco, or azure_stack_hci), `max_acceptable_rebuild_h` (hours per business SLA for returning to full protection after a class-A fault), `business_service` (runbook and CMDB id), `owner_email`, `change_freeze_until` (empty or timestamp through which you still log but can demote non-critical severities in parallel with finance close), `criticality_class` (A, B, C for how hard you page), and `expected_rebuild_throughput_gbps` (a planning floor, not a vendor limit, in GB per second of observed resync, used only for the `medium` bucket check).

Step 1 — Configure data collection

VMware (vCenter REST, Python 3, stdlib only, corporate CA) — the same `ssl.create_default_context` plus `load_verify_locations` pattern as the rest of the HCI pack. Authenticate to `POST /api/session` with Basic, store the `vmware-api-session-id` until expiry, and on `401` renew the session, on `429` or `503` or `504` back off and log the HTTP code so a silent poller does not look like a healthy array.

Poll `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/runtime-info` and post JSON one line per cluster with `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:cluster` so Splunk can read `current_fault_tolerance` and `policy_pftt`. Poll `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/resyncing-objects` and post to `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:resyncing` with `total_objects_resyncing`, `total_bytes_to_resync`, `eta_seconds`, and `resync_type` (`decommission`, `repair`, or `rebalance` depending on the API’s strings). Poll `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/health` and, client-side, keep rows where the test is `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.objecthealth`, emitting the four counters `healthy`, `nonAvailability`, `dataMove`, and `reduced` into `vmware:vsan:health` so the Step 2 `exp_obj` line has material to count.

vSAN HEC poller core (trim paths and secrets; same `ssl` and session pattern as the disk poller in UC-19.2.5): resolve clusters with `GET /api/vcenter/cluster?filter.vsan_enabled=true` or your static allow-list, then for each `cluster` id call the three vSAN paths and `hec_post` each JSON payload with a distinct `sourcetype` and `source` tag.

```python
import base64, json, ssl, sys, time, urllib.error, urllib.request

def hec_post(ctx, hec, token, event_obj, st, source, index_name):
    body = json.dumps(
        {
            "time": int(time.time()),
            "sourcetype": st,
            "source": source,
            "index": index_name,
            "event": json.dumps(event_obj, separators=(",", ":")),
        }
    ).encode("utf-8")
    req = urllib.request.Request(hec, data=body, method="POST")
    req.add_header("Authorization", "Splunk " + token)
    req.add_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=120) as r:
        r.read()

def vsphere_session(ctx, vcenter, user, password):
    tok = base64.b64encode((user + ":" + password).encode("utf-8")).decode("ascii")
    u = vcenter.rstrip("/") + "/api/session"
    req = urllib.request.Request(u, data=b"{}", method="POST")
    req.add_header("Authorization", "Basic " + tok)
    req.add_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=60) as r:
        return r.read().decode("utf-8").strip().strip('"')

def vsphere_get(ctx, vcenter, sid, path):
    u = vcenter.rstrip("/") + path
    req = urllib.request.Request(u, method="GET")
    req.add_header("vmware-api-session-id", sid)
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=120) as r:
        return r.read().decode("utf-8")

def main():
    vcenter, hec, hec_tok = "https://VCENTER", "https://IDX:8088/services/collector/event", "HEC-TOKEN"
    user, password, ca = "read-only@vsphere.local", "CHANGE_ME", "/etc/pki/enterprise/vcenter-ca.pem"
    index_name, ctx = "vmware", ssl.create_default_context()
    ctx.load_verify_locations(cafile=ca)
    try:
        sid = vsphere_session(ctx, vcenter, user, password)
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        if e.code == 401:
            sys.exit(2)
        raise
    try:
        clusters = json.loads(vsphere_get(ctx, vcenter, sid, "/api/vcenter/cluster?filter.vsan_enabled=true"))
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        if e.code in (401, 429, 503, 504):
            sys.exit(3)
        raise
    for c in clusters:
        cid = c.get("cluster")
        if not cid:
            continue
        p_resync = f"/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cid}/resyncing-objects"
        try:
            raw = vsphere_get(ctx, vcenter, sid, p_resync)
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code in (401, 429, 503, 504):
                time.sleep(5)
            continue
        if not raw:
            continue
        o = json.loads(raw)
        o["clusterId"] = cid
        o["api_path"] = "resyncing-objects"
        try:
            hec_post(
                ctx,
                hec,
                hec_tok,
                o,
                "vmware:vsan:resyncing",
                "vsan:resyncing_poll",
                index_name,
            )
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code in (401, 429, 503, 504):
                time.sleep(2)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

`vmware:vc:event` from Splunkbase 5380 should remain enabled on the vCenter forwarder path so rebuild start, component recovery, and completion strings land with normalised `EventType` fields for `rebuild_start` timing and for clearing a synthetic `rebuild_start_ts` when a completion event arrives.

Nutanix: install `Splunk_TA_nutanix` (5076) on a data collection node and Search Heads, enable inputs that target Prism Element or Prism Central per your support matrix, and confirm `nutanix:cluster` contains `data_resiliency_status`, `expected_replication_factor`, and `current_replication_factor` in `fieldsummary`. The TA does not ship a first-class Curator job API input; add a small HEC app `ta_nutanix_curator` that calls `/PrismGateway/services/rest/v1/curator/jobs` with the same `Authorization: Basic` header you already use in Prism, not the Element local account if Central requires SSO.

Cisco HyperFlex: create `cisco:hyperflex:cluster` by polling `GET /rest/clusters/{id}` with the same bearer or basic flow your HX team already approved, capturing `data_redundancy_state`, `replication_factor`, and the cluster `state` field. Add `cisco:hyperflex:alarm` as the alarm bus with titles mapped into JSON.

Azure Stack HCI: schedule a Windows task every 60 seconds on a management node, run PowerShell, convert `Get-StorageJob` to JSON, and POST to HEC with `sourcetype=azure_stack_hci:storage:repair`, fields `JobState`, `BytesProcessed`, `BytesTotal`, and `JobType` (`Regeneration`, `Repair`, or `Rebalance`).

`inputs.conf` and `props.conf` fragments (place in a customer app, not vendor defaults):

```ini
[http://HEC_hci]
disabled = 0
indexes = vmware, hci, nutanix, hyperflex, azhci
token = 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```

```ini
[vmware:vsan:resyncing]
TRUNCATE = 2000000
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TIME_PREFIX = "time"
```

```ini
[vmware:vsan:health]
TRUNCATE = 2000000
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
```

```ini
[nutanix:cluster:curator]
TRUNCATE = 2000000
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
```

```ini
[azure_stack_hci:storage:repair]
TRUNCATE = 2000000
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
```

Nutanix Curator HEC poller stub (std library only) — the TA still owns credentials in `password.conf`; the stub simply documents transport:

```python
import base64, json, ssl, time, urllib.request, urllib.error

def post_hec(ctx, hec_url, token, payload, sourcetype, index_name):
    body = json.dumps({
        "time": int(time.time()),
        "sourcetype": sourcetype,
        "source": "nutanix:curator_poll",
        "index": index_name,
        "event": json.dumps(payload),
    }).encode("utf-8")
    req = urllib.request.Request(hec_url, data=body, method="POST")
    req.add_header("Authorization", "Splunk " + token)
    req.add_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=60) as r:
        r.read()
```

`transforms.conf` should define `[rebuild_owner]` with `filename = rebuild_owner.csv` and `min_matches = 0`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The fenced SPL below is the same string as the `spl` field. It opens with a `comment` macro, constrains the interactive base to `earliest=-2h@h` on every multisearch sub-arm, and keeps AND/OR in uppercase in Boolean clauses as written. The first stage is `multisearch` so you do not have to pre-normalise at ingest time, only at search time, which is the usual compromise when HEC lines already vary between camel and snake. Each arm emits a small set of shared fields, then a global `sort` by `cluster_name` and `_time` so `streamstats` can look back one sample for a remaining-bytes delta. The `stats` that follows collapses to one engineering row per `(cluster_name, vendor)` and computes `rebuild_start` as the minimum of a vCenter event time or the earliest poll that still had active bytes. The `join` enforces `rebuild_owner` enrichment for SLA, owner, and freeze. The `case` call maps to five severities, then a freeze-aware remapping that never demotes a `critical` row. The `dedup` at the end follows the runbook in Step 5 for overlapping sourcetype double-counts.

Search walkthrough in plain terms: the multisearch block pulls every vendor you licensed into a single time-sorted set of rows; the `streamstats` pass estimates instantaneous throughput as the fall in remaining bytes between consecutive samples for a cluster; the `stats` pass collapses the stream to a single snapshot per cluster and vendor, computing how long the rebuild has been active and a smoothed rate over the first-to-last sample in the lookback; the `join` adds SLA, owner, and freeze policy; the `case` pass maps those facts into a severity; the `dedup` keeps the worst vendor row for each `cluster_name` if your feeds overlap. When you add a new vendor, you only add a fifth multisearch subsearch that emits the same field names, not a forked alert.


```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.9 — post-failure HCI resync: multisearch vmware+nutanix+cisco+azure; rebuild_owner; coalesce; streamstats throughput; five-tier; freeze never demotes critical; earliest=-2h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resyncing" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vc:event") earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, clusterId, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="vmware"
  | eval etype=if(sourcetype="vmware:vc:event",1,0)
  | eval bytes_rem=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_bytes_to_resync, totalBytesToResync, "0"))),0)
  | eval evraw=toString(coalesce(event_type, EventType, type, name, eventType, ""))
  | eval is_start=if(etype=1, if(match(evraw, "(?i)resync|rebuild|repair|decommission|rebalance|component|recover|begin|start|init"),1,0),0)
  | eval is_complete=if(etype=1, if(match(evraw, "(?i)complete|finish|succeed|ended|resynced"),1,0),0)
  | eval resync_t0=if(is_start=1, _time, null())
  | eval resync_t1=if(is_complete=1, _time, null())
  | eval tot_obj=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_objects_resyncing, totalObjectsResyncing, "0"))),0)
  | eval cur_ft=tonumber(toString(coalesce(current_fault_tolerance, currentFaultTolerance, "")))
  | eval pol_ft=tonumber(toString(coalesce(policy_pftt, policyPftt, "")))
  | eval test_id=toString(coalesce(test_id, testId, test, ""))
  | eval h_healthy=tonumber(toString(coalesce(healthy, "0")))
  | eval h_na2=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nonAvailability, non_availability, "0")))
  | eval h_move=tonumber(toString(coalesce(dataMove, "0")))
  | eval h_red=tonumber(toString(coalesce(reduced, "0")))
  | eval h_na1=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nonAvailabilityRelatedIncompliance, "0")))
  | eval h_na0=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nonAvailabilityRelatedReconfiguration, "0")))
  | eval is_objtest=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health", if(match(test_id, "(?i)objecthealth|com\\.vmware\\.vsan\\.health\\.test\\.objecthealth"),1,0),0)
  | eval exp_obj=if((is_objtest=1) OR (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resyncing" AND (tot_obj>0)), h_red + h_na2 + h_move + h_na0 + h_na1, 0)
  | eval data_resiliency=toString("OK")
  | eval cur_rf=tonumber("99")
  | eval exp_rf=tonumber("99")
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
    [ search (index=nutanix OR index=hci) (sourcetype="nutanix:cluster" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:curator") earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="nutanix"
  | eval etype=0
  | eval bytes_rem=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_to_replicate, bytesToReplicate, unprotected_bytes, unprotectedBytes, "0"))),0)
  | eval resync_t0=null()
  | eval resync_t1=null()
  | eval tot_obj=0
  | eval data_resiliency=toString(coalesce(data_resiliency_status, dataResiliencyStatus, "OK"))
  | eval exp_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(expected_replication_factor, expectedReplicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval cur_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(current_replication_factor, currentReplicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval pol_ft=if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>0), max(exp_rf-1,0), null())
  | eval cur_ft=if((isnotnull(cur_rf)) AND (isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (cur_rf<exp_rf), 0, if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>0), max(exp_rf-1,0), null()))
  | eval h_healthy=0
  | eval exp_obj=0
  | eval is_start=0
  | eval is_complete=0
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
    [ search (index=hyperflex OR index=hci) (sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm") earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, displayName, cluster_id, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="cisco"
  | eval etype=0
  | eval bytes_rem=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(unprotected_bytes, unprotectedBytes, bytes_stale, "0"))),0)
  | eval resync_t0=null()
  | eval resync_t1=null()
  | eval tot_obj=0
  | eval data_resiliency=toString(coalesce(data_redundancy_state, dataRedundancyState, "OK"))
  | eval exp_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(replication_factor, replicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval cur_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(replication_factor, replicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval pol_ft=if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>1), exp_rf-1, null())
  | eval cur_ft=if((match(lower(tostring(data_resiliency)), "(?i)degrad|rebuild|resync|remirror|replica.*loss")) AND (isnotnull(pol_ft)), if((isnotnull(pol_ft)) AND (pol_ft>0), pol_ft-1, 0), pol_ft)
  | eval h_healthy=0
  | eval exp_obj=if(sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm", 1, 0)
  | eval is_start=0
  | eval is_complete=0
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
    [ search (index=azhci OR index=hci) sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:storage:repair" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ClusterName, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
  | eval etype=0
  | eval btot=tonumber(toString(coalesce(BytesTotal, bytesTotal, "0")))
  | eval bproc=tonumber(toString(coalesce(BytesProcessed, bytesProcessed, "0")))
  | eval jstate=toString(coalesce(JobState, jobState, job_state, "Completed"))
  | eval bytes_rem=if(match(lower(jstate), "(?i)running|suspended|inprogress|queued"), max(btot - bproc, 0), 0)
  | eval resync_t0=if(match(lower(jstate), "(?i)running|suspended|inprogress|queued"), _time, null())
  | eval resync_t1=if(match(lower(jstate), "(?i)complete|succeeded"), _time, null())
  | eval tot_obj=0
  | eval data_resiliency=if((bytes_rem>0), "kCriticalAttentionNeeded", "OK")
  | eval exp_rf=2
  | eval cur_rf=2
  | eval pol_ft=1
  | eval cur_ft=if((bytes_rem>0), 0, 1)
  | eval h_healthy=0
  | eval exp_obj=0
  | eval is_start=0
  | eval is_complete=0
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
| sort 0 cluster_name, _time
| streamstats current=f last(bytes_rem) as prev_bytes last(_time) as prev_t by cluster_name
| eval sec_span=max(_time - prev_t, 0.0001)
| eval inst_thr_gbps=max((max(prev_bytes,0) - max(bytes_rem,0)), 0) / 1000000000 / max(sec_span, 0.0001)
| stats
    latest(bytes_rem) as bytes_rem
    max(inst_thr_gbps) as inst_thr
    first(prev_bytes) as _first_prev
    first(prev_t) as _first_t
    last(_time) as _last_t
    max(tot_obj) as tot_obj
    latest(cur_ft) as current_ft
    latest(pol_ft) as pol_ft
    latest(data_resiliency) as data_resiliency_status
    latest(cur_rf) as cur_rf
    latest(exp_rf) as exp_rf
    max(exp_obj) as exposed_objects_count
    max(h_healthy) as h_healthy
    min(eval(if(((bytes_rem>0) OR (tot_obj>0) OR (match(tostring(data_resiliency), "(?i)kFailure|kCritical|degrad|rebuild|Running|Suspended|Attention"))), _time, null()))) as t_active
    min(eval(if((isnotnull(resync_t0) AND (resync_t0>0)), resync_t0, null()))) as t_start_ev
  BY cluster_name, vendor
| eval rebuild_start=coalesce(t_start_ev, t_active)
| eval time_in_rebuild_h=if((isnotnull(rebuild_start)) AND ((bytes_rem>0) OR (tot_obj>0) OR match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "(?i)kFailure|kCritical|degrad|Attention|Running|Suspended|rebuild")), round((now()-rebuild_start)/3600, 3), 0)
| eval thr_window_s=max(_last_t - _first_t, 0.0001)
| eval thr_smooth=if((isnotnull(_first_prev)) AND (isnotnull(_first_t)) AND (isnotnull(_last_t)) AND (thr_window_s>0), max((tonumber(_first_prev) - tonumber(bytes_rem)),0) / 1000000000 / thr_window_s, 0.000001)
| eval thr_use=max(inst_thr, thr_smooth)
| eval expected_ft=if(isnotnull(pol_ft) AND (pol_ft>=0), pol_ft, if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>0), exp_rf-1, null()))
| eval expected_fault_tolerance=if(isnotnull(expected_ft), expected_ft, 1)
| eval eta_s=if((bytes_rem>0) AND (thr_use>0.0000001), (bytes_rem / 1000000000) / thr_use, 0)
| eval eta_h=if((isnotnull(eta_s) AND (eta_s>0)), round(eta_s/3600, 2), 0)
| eval bytes_to_resync_gb=round(bytes_rem/1000000000, 3)
| eval throughput_gbps=round(thr_use, 4)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup rebuild_owner | eval cluster_name=toString(cluster_name) | fields cluster_name, vendor, max_acceptable_rebuild_h, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, criticality_class, expected_rebuild_throughput_gbps ]
| eval max_acceptable_rebuild_h=tonumber(toString(coalesce(max_acceptable_rebuild_h, 24)))
| eval e_thr=tonumber(toString(coalesce(expected_rebuild_throughput_gbps, 0.5)))
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft)) AND (now()<cft), 1, 0)
| eval ft_breach=if((isnotnull(current_ft) AND (isnotnull(expected_fault_tolerance)) AND (current_ft<expected_fault_tolerance)),1,0)
| eval sev0=case(
  (data_resiliency_status="kFailureDetected") OR (current_ft=0) OR (time_in_rebuild_h>(2*max_acceptable_rebuild_h)),
  "critical",
  (time_in_rebuild_h>max_acceptable_rebuild_h) OR (exposed_objects_count>0) OR (ft_breach=1) OR (match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "(?i)kCriticalAttentionNeeded")),
  "high",
  ((bytes_to_resync_gb>0) OR (tot_obj>0) OR (match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "(?i)Attention|Risk|Degrad"))) AND (throughput_gbps>=e_thr),
  "medium",
  ((bytes_to_resync_gb>0) OR (tot_obj>0)) AND (time_in_rebuild_h<(10/60)),
  "low",
  1=1,
  "ok"
)
| eval sev0=if((isnull(sev0)) OR (sev0=""), "ok", sev0)
| eval sev0=if(((bytes_to_resync_gb=0) AND (tot_obj=0) AND (match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "^(OK|kOK)"))), "ok", sev0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=0), sev0, if((sev0="critical"), "critical", if((sev0="high"), "medium", if((sev0="medium"), "low", if((sev0="low"), "ok", if((sev0="ok"), "ok", sev0))))))
| eval sevord=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="high",2, severity_bucket="medium",3, severity_bucket="low",4, severity_bucket="ok",5,1=1,6)
| table cluster_name, vendor, data_resiliency_status, bytes_to_resync_gb, throughput_gbps, eta_h, time_in_rebuild_h, max_acceptable_rebuild_h, exposed_objects_count, current_ft, expected_ft, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, sevord
| sort 0 sevord, -time_in_rebuild_h, cluster_name
| dedup cluster_name
| fields - sevord

```

Schedule the alert on a 5- or 15-minute cron, use the `cluster_name` field for throttling, and send `severity_bucket` plus `time_in_rebuild_h` in the body so a human can compare the SLA from `max_acceptable_rebuild_h` without re-opening the lookup.

Step 3 — Validate

(1) VMware: vSphere Client, pick the vSAN cluster, then Monitor, vSAN, Resyncing Objects; compare `total bytes to resync` and the ETA to `bytes_to_resync_gb` and `eta_h` in Splunk for the same 10-minute window. (2) Nutanix: Prism, Home, Data Resiliency tile, then Hardware, Diagram, Curator job history, and compare the alert titles in Splunk to `nutanix:cluster:alerts` rows. (3) HyperFlex: HX Connect, System Information, Resiliency, plus the alarm drawer for the same `cluster_id`. (4) Azure: Windows Admin Center, Cluster, Storage, Volumes, Repair jobs; compare to `Get-StorageJob` JSON you POSTed. (5) cURL: `curl -k -H "vmware-api-session-id: $SID" "https://vcenter/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/resyncing-objects"`; Nutanix `curl -k -u user:pass "https://prism:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v1/curator/jobs"`; Cisco `curl -k -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOK" "https://hx/rest/clusters/{id}"` so network and role issues are not mistaken for a math bug.

When Splunk disagrees with a UI, first check that `earliest` windows overlap. Second, remember Curator is a planner, Stargate is an executor, so a zero Curator list with a non-zero vSAN resyncing count can still be true if the poll cadences differ. Third, Azure jobs disappear the moment a job is `Complete`, so Splunk will show a gap with no rows even though the volume is healthy, which is why you pair event logs for long historical analytics.

This validation path is not the disk inventory proof from UC-19.2.5, not the failure-domain topology proof from UC-19.2.16, and not the capacity free-space check from UC-19.2.27.

Step 4 — Operationalise

Row A — two single value panels: count of `cluster_name` with `bytes_to_resync_gb>0` and count where `current_ft=0` from the last run. Row B — a table of the Step 2 fields with a colour field on `severity_bucket`. Row C — a `timechart` of `max(throughput_gbps)` per `cluster_name` to show throttling. Row D — a scatter of `bytes_to_resync_gb` on X and `eta_h` on Y to spot stalled work in the top-right. Route PagerDuty for `current_ft=0` or `severity_bucket=critical`, open ServiceNow P1 for `time_in_rebuild_h` greater than `max_acceptable_rebuild_h` when `freeze_active=0`, Slack for `medium` during business hours, and email for `low`. ITSI: one service per `cluster_name`, KPIs for a numeric resiliency code (`0=ok`, `1=degraded`, `2=failure` mapped from `data_resiliency_status`), `time_in_rebuild_h`, and `throughput_gbps` with adaptive thresholds. Runbook titles: (a) rebuild stalled — look for network back-pressure and foreground I/O caps; (b) ETA over SLA with healthy throughput — engage vendor TAC and finance for temporary nodes; (c) `current_ft=0` with low throughput — invoke emergency evacuation to another cluster and freeze changes until a root cause is set.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(a) Empty table — verify HEC token, confirm each sourcetype with `tstats` counts, and curl the vSAN `resyncing-objects` path from the same host as the poller. (b) `bytes_to_resync` is zero on a hot cluster — vSAN can briefly report a zero with active health stress; re-read `vmware:vsan:health` object counts. (c) ETA is enormous at minute zero — the first `streamstats` pair has a tiny delta; widen schedule or add a pre-summary job. (d) Curator count stuck at zero — you pointed the Curator call at a Prism Central SSO endpoint without the local service account, so rotate the credential that has `/curator` rights. (e) `data_redundancy_state` says `Online` but alarms still list rebuild — REST state often follows completion; use `cisco:hyperflex:alarm` as the time truth. (f) `Get-StorageJob` is empty on Azure — jobs are only present while `Running` or `Suspended`, so add `Get-WinEvent` shipping for Storage Spaces health channel if you need historical joins. (g) Freeze appears to down-class a `kFailureDetected` line — the `case` list forces `critical` to stay, so if you still see a demotion, you copied a legacy `if` that omits the explicit `sev0="critical"` arm; re-open the Step 2 fence. (h) You see two rows for one `cluster_name` — `dedup cluster_name` after sorting by a severity order, as written. (i) Per-vendor field map: vSAN `total_bytes_to_resync`, Nutanix `bytes_to_replicate` in some builds, HyperFlex `unprotected_bytes`, Azure `BytesTotal-BytesProcessed`. (j) `time_in_rebuild_h` still climbs after a clean UI — a missing completion event means `rebuild_start` never reset; add a vSAN completion event to the `is_complete` matcher or clear on zero bytes. (k) Extending to Dell APEX with Azure or NetApp vSphere builds — add their public REST or PowerShell entry points in a new multisearch subsearch with the same output fields, not a fork of the severity math, and file a sibling UC for PowerStore specific APIs when you have stable field names. (l) Throughput bounces to zero on healthy clusters with no rebuild — the poller may be on a 60s cadence while the UI averages five minutes, which is a sampling mismatch, not a free lunch on risk.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.9 — post-failure HCI resync: multisearch vmware+nutanix+cisco+azure; rebuild_owner; coalesce; streamstats throughput; five-tier; freeze never demotes critical; earliest=-2h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resyncing" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vc:event") earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, clusterId, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="vmware"
  | eval etype=if(sourcetype="vmware:vc:event",1,0)
  | eval bytes_rem=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_bytes_to_resync, totalBytesToResync, "0"))),0)
  | eval evraw=toString(coalesce(event_type, EventType, type, name, eventType, ""))
  | eval is_start=if(etype=1, if(match(evraw, "(?i)resync|rebuild|repair|decommission|rebalance|component|recover|begin|start|init"),1,0),0)
  | eval is_complete=if(etype=1, if(match(evraw, "(?i)complete|finish|succeed|ended|resynced"),1,0),0)
  | eval resync_t0=if(is_start=1, _time, null())
  | eval resync_t1=if(is_complete=1, _time, null())
  | eval tot_obj=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_objects_resyncing, totalObjectsResyncing, "0"))),0)
  | eval cur_ft=tonumber(toString(coalesce(current_fault_tolerance, currentFaultTolerance, "")))
  | eval pol_ft=tonumber(toString(coalesce(policy_pftt, policyPftt, "")))
  | eval test_id=toString(coalesce(test_id, testId, test, ""))
  | eval h_healthy=tonumber(toString(coalesce(healthy, "0")))
  | eval h_na2=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nonAvailability, non_availability, "0")))
  | eval h_move=tonumber(toString(coalesce(dataMove, "0")))
  | eval h_red=tonumber(toString(coalesce(reduced, "0")))
  | eval h_na1=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nonAvailabilityRelatedIncompliance, "0")))
  | eval h_na0=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nonAvailabilityRelatedReconfiguration, "0")))
  | eval is_objtest=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health", if(match(test_id, "(?i)objecthealth|com\\.vmware\\.vsan\\.health\\.test\\.objecthealth"),1,0),0)
  | eval exp_obj=if((is_objtest=1) OR (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resyncing" AND (tot_obj>0)), h_red + h_na2 + h_move + h_na0 + h_na1, 0)
  | eval data_resiliency=toString("OK")
  | eval cur_rf=tonumber("99")
  | eval exp_rf=tonumber("99")
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
    [ search (index=nutanix OR index=hci) (sourcetype="nutanix:cluster" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:curator") earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="nutanix"
  | eval etype=0
  | eval bytes_rem=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_to_replicate, bytesToReplicate, unprotected_bytes, unprotectedBytes, "0"))),0)
  | eval resync_t0=null()
  | eval resync_t1=null()
  | eval tot_obj=0
  | eval data_resiliency=toString(coalesce(data_resiliency_status, dataResiliencyStatus, "OK"))
  | eval exp_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(expected_replication_factor, expectedReplicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval cur_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(current_replication_factor, currentReplicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval pol_ft=if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>0), max(exp_rf-1,0), null())
  | eval cur_ft=if((isnotnull(cur_rf)) AND (isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (cur_rf<exp_rf), 0, if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>0), max(exp_rf-1,0), null()))
  | eval h_healthy=0
  | eval exp_obj=0
  | eval is_start=0
  | eval is_complete=0
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
    [ search (index=hyperflex OR index=hci) (sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm") earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, displayName, cluster_id, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="cisco"
  | eval etype=0
  | eval bytes_rem=round(tonumber(toString(coalesce(unprotected_bytes, unprotectedBytes, bytes_stale, "0"))),0)
  | eval resync_t0=null()
  | eval resync_t1=null()
  | eval tot_obj=0
  | eval data_resiliency=toString(coalesce(data_redundancy_state, dataRedundancyState, "OK"))
  | eval exp_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(replication_factor, replicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval cur_rf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(replication_factor, replicationFactor, "0")))
  | eval pol_ft=if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>1), exp_rf-1, null())
  | eval cur_ft=if((match(lower(tostring(data_resiliency)), "(?i)degrad|rebuild|resync|remirror|replica.*loss")) AND (isnotnull(pol_ft)), if((isnotnull(pol_ft)) AND (pol_ft>0), pol_ft-1, 0), pol_ft)
  | eval h_healthy=0
  | eval exp_obj=if(sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm", 1, 0)
  | eval is_start=0
  | eval is_complete=0
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
    [ search (index=azhci OR index=hci) sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:storage:repair" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, ClusterName, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
  | eval etype=0
  | eval btot=tonumber(toString(coalesce(BytesTotal, bytesTotal, "0")))
  | eval bproc=tonumber(toString(coalesce(BytesProcessed, bytesProcessed, "0")))
  | eval jstate=toString(coalesce(JobState, jobState, job_state, "Completed"))
  | eval bytes_rem=if(match(lower(jstate), "(?i)running|suspended|inprogress|queued"), max(btot - bproc, 0), 0)
  | eval resync_t0=if(match(lower(jstate), "(?i)running|suspended|inprogress|queued"), _time, null())
  | eval resync_t1=if(match(lower(jstate), "(?i)complete|succeeded"), _time, null())
  | eval tot_obj=0
  | eval data_resiliency=if((bytes_rem>0), "kCriticalAttentionNeeded", "OK")
  | eval exp_rf=2
  | eval cur_rf=2
  | eval pol_ft=1
  | eval cur_ft=if((bytes_rem>0), 0, 1)
  | eval h_healthy=0
  | eval exp_obj=0
  | eval is_start=0
  | eval is_complete=0
| fields _time, cluster_name, vendor, bytes_rem, resync_t0, resync_t1, tot_obj, cur_ft, pol_ft, h_healthy, exp_obj, data_resiliency, cur_rf, exp_rf, is_start, is_complete, etype, sourcetype, test_id
]
| sort 0 cluster_name, _time
| streamstats current=f last(bytes_rem) as prev_bytes last(_time) as prev_t by cluster_name
| eval sec_span=max(_time - prev_t, 0.0001)
| eval inst_thr_gbps=max((max(prev_bytes,0) - max(bytes_rem,0)), 0) / 1000000000 / max(sec_span, 0.0001)
| stats
    latest(bytes_rem) as bytes_rem
    max(inst_thr_gbps) as inst_thr
    first(prev_bytes) as _first_prev
    first(prev_t) as _first_t
    last(_time) as _last_t
    max(tot_obj) as tot_obj
    latest(cur_ft) as current_ft
    latest(pol_ft) as pol_ft
    latest(data_resiliency) as data_resiliency_status
    latest(cur_rf) as cur_rf
    latest(exp_rf) as exp_rf
    max(exp_obj) as exposed_objects_count
    max(h_healthy) as h_healthy
    min(eval(if(((bytes_rem>0) OR (tot_obj>0) OR (match(tostring(data_resiliency), "(?i)kFailure|kCritical|degrad|rebuild|Running|Suspended|Attention"))), _time, null()))) as t_active
    min(eval(if((isnotnull(resync_t0) AND (resync_t0>0)), resync_t0, null()))) as t_start_ev
  BY cluster_name, vendor
| eval rebuild_start=coalesce(t_start_ev, t_active)
| eval time_in_rebuild_h=if((isnotnull(rebuild_start)) AND ((bytes_rem>0) OR (tot_obj>0) OR match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "(?i)kFailure|kCritical|degrad|Attention|Running|Suspended|rebuild")), round((now()-rebuild_start)/3600, 3), 0)
| eval thr_window_s=max(_last_t - _first_t, 0.0001)
| eval thr_smooth=if((isnotnull(_first_prev)) AND (isnotnull(_first_t)) AND (isnotnull(_last_t)) AND (thr_window_s>0), max((tonumber(_first_prev) - tonumber(bytes_rem)),0) / 1000000000 / thr_window_s, 0.000001)
| eval thr_use=max(inst_thr, thr_smooth)
| eval expected_ft=if(isnotnull(pol_ft) AND (pol_ft>=0), pol_ft, if((isnotnull(exp_rf)) AND (exp_rf>0), exp_rf-1, null()))
| eval expected_fault_tolerance=if(isnotnull(expected_ft), expected_ft, 1)
| eval eta_s=if((bytes_rem>0) AND (thr_use>0.0000001), (bytes_rem / 1000000000) / thr_use, 0)
| eval eta_h=if((isnotnull(eta_s) AND (eta_s>0)), round(eta_s/3600, 2), 0)
| eval bytes_to_resync_gb=round(bytes_rem/1000000000, 3)
| eval throughput_gbps=round(thr_use, 4)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup rebuild_owner | eval cluster_name=toString(cluster_name) | fields cluster_name, vendor, max_acceptable_rebuild_h, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, criticality_class, expected_rebuild_throughput_gbps ]
| eval max_acceptable_rebuild_h=tonumber(toString(coalesce(max_acceptable_rebuild_h, 24)))
| eval e_thr=tonumber(toString(coalesce(expected_rebuild_throughput_gbps, 0.5)))
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft)) AND (now()<cft), 1, 0)
| eval ft_breach=if((isnotnull(current_ft) AND (isnotnull(expected_fault_tolerance)) AND (current_ft<expected_fault_tolerance)),1,0)
| eval sev0=case(
  (data_resiliency_status="kFailureDetected") OR (current_ft=0) OR (time_in_rebuild_h>(2*max_acceptable_rebuild_h)),
  "critical",
  (time_in_rebuild_h>max_acceptable_rebuild_h) OR (exposed_objects_count>0) OR (ft_breach=1) OR (match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "(?i)kCriticalAttentionNeeded")),
  "high",
  ((bytes_to_resync_gb>0) OR (tot_obj>0) OR (match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "(?i)Attention|Risk|Degrad"))) AND (throughput_gbps>=e_thr),
  "medium",
  ((bytes_to_resync_gb>0) OR (tot_obj>0)) AND (time_in_rebuild_h<(10/60)),
  "low",
  1=1,
  "ok"
)
| eval sev0=if((isnull(sev0)) OR (sev0=""), "ok", sev0)
| eval sev0=if(((bytes_to_resync_gb=0) AND (tot_obj=0) AND (match(tostring(data_resiliency_status), "^(OK|kOK)"))), "ok", sev0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=0), sev0, if((sev0="critical"), "critical", if((sev0="high"), "medium", if((sev0="medium"), "low", if((sev0="low"), "ok", if((sev0="ok"), "ok", sev0))))))
| eval sevord=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="high",2, severity_bucket="medium",3, severity_bucket="low",4, severity_bucket="ok",5,1=1,6)
| table cluster_name, vendor, data_resiliency_status, bytes_to_resync_gb, throughput_gbps, eta_h, time_in_rebuild_h, max_acceptable_rebuild_h, exposed_objects_count, current_ft, expected_ft, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, sevord
| sort 0 sevord, -time_in_rebuild_h, cluster_name
| dedup cluster_name
| fields - sevord

```

## Visualization

Single value: clusters with an active resync. Single value: clusters with `current_ft=0` from the latest run. Table: Step 2 output fields with colour on `severity_bucket`. Timechart: `max(throughput_gbps)` by `cluster_name`. Scatter: `bytes_to_resync_gb` versus `eta_h` to spot stuck rebuilds in the long-ETA, high-remaining quadrant.

## References

- [vSphere 8+ — vCenter REST, vsan resyncing-objects](https://developer.broadcom.com/xapis/vsphere-automation-api/latest/)
- [Nutanix — Developer portal (Prism, APIs, and automation reference)](https://www.nutanix.dev/)
- [Cisco HyperFlex — HX Data Platform API reference (clusters REST)](https://developer.cisco.com/docs/hyperflex-data-platform-api/)
- [Microsoft Learn — Get-StorageJob (Storage Spaces and Azure Stack HCI operations)](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/module/storage/get-storagejob)
